title: Minor improvements and fixes
description: Feature root for small improvements and bug fixes that don't warrant their own feature branch.

handoff_summary.completed: Reviewed codebase for improvements, validated existing child beads, identified dependency ordering issue for TUI beads, proposed one additional bead.
handoff_summary.remaining: Child beads need dependency updates: B0130 should depend on B0131, B0132 should depend on B0130. New bead B0133 (git error context) needs creation.
handoff_summary.risks: B0130, B0131, B0132 all modify tui.py — without dependencies they will block each other at schedule time due to file-scope conflict detection. B0131 is the largest TUI change (async scheduler + status panel refactor) and should go first to establish the new async foundation.
handoff_summary.next_action: Update dependencies on existing beads: B0130 depends on B0131, B0132 depends on B0130. Create B0133 for git error context improvement. Then run scheduler to execute B0129 and B0131 in parallel.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: B0130, B0131, B0132 all touch src/codex_orchestrator/tui.py — must be sequenced via dependencies

conflict_risks: B0130, B0131, B0132 all touch src/codex_orchestrator/tui.py — must be sequenced via dependencies

execution_history.summary: Reviewed B0128 feature root and its 4 existing child beads (B0129-B0132). The existing decomposition covers telemetry fix (B0129), TUI tree widget (B0130), async scheduler (B0131), and telemetry display (B0132). Critical finding: B0130, B0131, B0132 all claim tui.py with no inter-dependencies, which will cause file-scope conflicts. Recommended dependency chain: B0131 → B0130 → B0132. Proposing one additional bead for improving git error context in gitutils.py.

guardrails.template_text:
# Planner Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Decompose a feature into a parent epic and actionable child beads with clear dependencies and scope.

Allowed actions:
- Read specifications and repository context.
- Propose bead structure, dependencies, linked docs, and expected file scope.
- Create follow-up planning work when decomposition is blocked by missing information.

Disallowed actions:
- Implement code, tests, docs, or runtime behavior changes.
- Claim work outside planning scope as completed.

Expected outputs:
- Structured planning JSON matching the planner schema.
- Beads with concise, role-appropriate acceptance criteria and file scope.
